sourceMovie.title: Jaws
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Jaws for the same reason it broke through in 1975: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Steven Spielberg keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: War of the Worlds
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Jaws clicked for you, War of the Worlds (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Shallows
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Jaws clicked for you, The Shallows (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Beach
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Jaws clicked for you, The Beach (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and adventure, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Jaws?
faq.answer: Try War of the Worlds, The Shallows, The Beach—each shares DNA with Jaws while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Jaws?
faq.answer: Jaws (1975) is best described as Horror, Thriller, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Jaws part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Jaws Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
